repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: UPRISE_NEXT is a pnpm monorepo. The primary authority order is AGENTS.md, then docs/canon, then active docs/specs, then active founder locks/execution docs in docs/solutions, then current branch runtime/code evidence, then dated handoffs. The generated wiki must not treat legacy docs, stale prompt packs, or historical handoffs as equal authority.

repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: This repo contains both current active MVP doctrine and a large amount of legacy/historical documentation. Deprioritize docs/legacy/** and older mixed-era prompt packs unless a newer doc explicitly points back to them. When sources conflict, separate current active MVP lock, current runtime reality, and legacy/later-version carry-forward instead of flattening them.

repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: Prioritize the current MVP action grammar and surface doctrine from docs/solutions/MVP_ACTION_SYSTEM_MATRIX_R1.md, MVP_DISCOVER_FOUNDER_LOCK_R1.md, MVP_ARTIST_PROFILE_FOUNDER_LOCK_R1.md, MVP_SOURCE_AND_FEED_RULES_FOUNDER_LOCK_R1.md, and MVP_STATS_FOUNDER_LOCK_R1.md. Recent carry-forward is concentrated in docs/handoff/2026-04-16_notebooklm_artist-profile-and-dashboard_briefing.md and related 2026-04-16/2026-04-17 handoffs.

repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: Key architecture areas: apps/web is the Next.js web client and must respect WEB_TIER_BOUNDARY.md; apps/api is the NestJS API; packages/types contains shared public contracts and is important for generated documentation; apps/socket exists for realtime transport. Current MVP is local-community-first, with live Discover deferred and discovery/statistics currently flowing through intermittent feed inserts rather than a full live discover destination.

repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: Treat external models as separate departments, not separate authorities. Design agents can explore layout but cannot redefine action grammar. Writing/briefing agents can summarize and draft but cannot invent doctrine. Coding agents must implement from locks/specs/runtime. The wiki should help those departments orient quickly without letting any of them replace repo truth.

repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: For section-specific work, generated wiki pages should point agents to docs/agent-briefs/ first. Section briefs are routers: read the matching brief, then selectively load only the code/spec/lock files needed for the task. Do not make agents bulk-read every doc that mentions the same surface.

repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: Generated wiki pages should treat docs/agent-briefs/CONTEXT_ROUTER.md as the work-lane map. Use it to select the active focus lane first, then load only the lane brief and companion briefs touched by the task.

repo_notes.author: product engineering
repo_notes.content: For canon-sensitive work, generated wiki pages should not bulk-load every canon file. Use docs/agent-briefs/CONTEXT_ROUTER.md to select the exact canon anchor for the domain: narrative mechanics, glossary, identity/philosophy, surfaces/capabilities, revenue, voice/messaging, or project orientation.

pages.title: UPRISE_NEXT Overview
pages.purpose: Explain what the UPRISE_NEXT repo is, how the monorepo is organized, and what the current active MVP focus is.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Use docs/architecture/UPRISE_OVERVIEW.md, docs/PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md, and docs/solutions/EXTERNAL_ASSISTANT_REPO_BRIEF_R1.md as primary inputs. Mention apps/web, apps/api, apps/socket, and packages/types explicitly.

pages.title: Authority And Documentation Rules
pages.purpose: Document the repo's authority order, how to read docs safely, what outranks what, and how to separate current locks from runtime and legacy material.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Anchor this page on AGENTS.md, docs/AGENT_STRATEGY_AND_HANDOFF.md, docs/agent-briefs/CONTEXT_ROUTER.md, docs/agent-briefs/README.md, docs/canon/, and docs/solutions/REPO_AUTHORITY_MAP_R1.md. This page should make it hard for agents to treat stale docs as current truth, bulk-load every related document by default, or miss the relevant canon anchor.

pages.title: Context Router And Section Briefs
pages.purpose: Explain how agents should use docs/agent-briefs/CONTEXT_ROUTER.md and section briefs as narrow work-lane routers instead of bulk-loading every related solution, handoff, or design document.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Use docs/agent-briefs/CONTEXT_ROUTER.md, docs/agent-briefs/README.md, docs/agent-briefs/UI_CURRENT.md, docs/agent-briefs/ACTIONS_AND_SIGNALS.md, docs/agent-briefs/ARTIST_PROFILE_SOURCE_DASHBOARD.md, docs/agent-briefs/EVENTS_ARCHIVE.md, docs/canon/, and docs/AGENT_STRATEGY_AND_HANDOFF.md. Emphasize active focus lanes, exact canon anchors, brief-first loading, selective companion reads, and the rule that section briefs summarize active working truth without replacing owning specs or founder locks.

pages.title: Architecture And Tier Boundaries
pages.purpose: Document the system architecture, app/package boundaries, and especially the web-tier boundary and shared contract layers.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Prioritize apps/web/WEB_TIER_BOUNDARY.md, docs/architecture/UPRISE_OVERVIEW.md, docs/PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md, packages/types, apps/web/src, and apps/api/src.

pages.title: Current MVP Action Grammar
pages.purpose: Explain the current active action system, including what actions exist now, what they apply to, and which older action terms are stale or derived-state only.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Use docs/solutions/MVP_ACTION_SYSTEM_MATRIX_R1.md as the primary authority. Make sure this page distinguishes direct actions from derived state and reflects current founder-lock doctrine rather than old prompt-pack language.

pages.title: Listening Modes And Surface Separation
pages.purpose: Document the current separation between broadcast listening, personal collection listening, artist-profile demo listening, and deferred discovery surfaces.

pages.title: Web Routes And Surface Contracts
pages.purpose: Explain the main web surfaces, their roles, and where current route behavior is locked versus deferred.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Prioritize docs/agent-briefs/CONTEXT_ROUTER.md to identify the active work lane, then docs/agent-briefs/UI_CURRENT.md for current UI orientation, then Plot, Discover, Community, and Artist Profile route behavior as needed. Use active surface contracts and current founder locks instead of stale route descriptions.

pages.title: Artist Profile And Source Surfaces
pages.purpose: Document the current intended artist-profile behavior, current runtime lag, and how source surfaces differ from broadcast and collection listening contexts.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Use docs/solutions/MVP_ARTIST_PROFILE_FOUNDER_LOCK_R1.md, docs/handoff/2026-04-16_notebooklm_artist-profile-and-dashboard_briefing.md, and apps/web/src/app/artist-bands/[id]/page.tsx. Distinguish locked doctrine from current live implementation.

pages.title: API And Shared Contracts
pages.purpose: Document where shared types and public contracts live, how the API and web coordinate, and which packages are central to agent understanding.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Focus on packages/types, apps/api/src, and apps/web clients. Make clear that shared types and API contracts matter more than inferred UI text when documenting runtime behavior.

pages.title: Fixture And QA Setup
pages.purpose: Explain how deterministic fixtures and QA setup currently work for artist profiles, feed/discovery stats, and account-type testing.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Use apps/api/scripts/seed-artist-fixture-roster.mjs and docs/handoff/2026-04-16_artist-fixture-roster-seed.md. Mention that listener-only fixtures exist and why they matter.

pages.title: Deferred And Legacy Domains
pages.purpose: Make clear which parts of the repo are intentionally deferred, which are later-version carry-forward, and which directories/docs are legacy reference only.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: This page should explicitly call out docs/legacy/** and older prompt packs as lower-priority reference material. It should also explain that some current doctrine is locked before implementation is complete.

pages.title: External Assistant Operating Model
pages.purpose: Document how external assistants such as Abacus, NotebookLM, and design agents should be used against this repo and how they are divided into departmental roles.

page_notes.author: product engineering
page_notes.content: Use docs/solutions/EXTERNAL_ASSISTANT_REPO_BRIEF_R1.md, docs/solutions/REPO_AUTHORITY_MAP_R1.md, docs/solutions/AGENT_WIKI_STEERING_R1.md, docs/agent-briefs/CONTEXT_ROUTER.md, and docs/agent-briefs/README.md. Emphasize department-style usage, focus-lane routing, brief-first task loading, and no authority over repo truth.
